title: Althaea maritima
taxonomy: Althaea maritima is classified as a member of the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow family. It belongs to the genus Althaea, which includes several species of marshmallow plants. Althaea maritima is native to certain regions and is often found in salt marshes and coastal areas. This classification places Althaea maritima within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Further classification includes its kingdom as Plantae.
uses: Althaea maritima has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety may not be scientifically proven. Althaea maritima mucilage is thought to have soothing and protective properties, which could potentially be used to treat gastrointestinal issues. Some studies suggest that the plant's mucilage may also have anti-inflammatory effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its medicinal uses. Althaea maritima is also used in some cosmetics and skincare products, possibly due to its mucilage's emollient properties.

Althaea maritima contains active constituents such as mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. preparations: Althaea maritima, a coastal species of marsh mallow, has been used in traditional herbal medicine to prepare various remedies. intro: Althaea maritima, also known as the marsh mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, characterized by its tall stems and showy, pale purple flowers. The health benefits of Althaea maritima include its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include mucilages, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Althaea maritima is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, as well as in traditional remedies like marshmallow root extract and marshmallow syrup.
